title: T-003 — Migration: item_cost_history table
prompt:
# T-003 — Migration: item_cost_history table

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §5.3, §11.1, §11.2
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Cost history table with effective dates, source attribution, RLS.

currency stays CHAR(3) — ISO 4217 validated at API layer (V-TEC-52). source CHECK enforces the four allowed enum values. Do not back-fill items.cost_per_kg — that runs in T-021 (cost API).

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- db/migrations/0xxx_item_cost_history.sql [create]
- db/schema/cost-history.ts [create]
- tests/db/cost.migration.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Cost API endpoints
- UI

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration applied, when describing item_cost_history, then all 9 columns and CHECK on source exist.
2. Given two cost rows for same item, when querying with idx_item_cost_active, then ordering is effective_from DESC.
3. Given RLS enabled, when no org context, then SELECT returns 0 rows.

## Test strategy
- RED test: insert two rows for same item across orgs and assert isolation under RLS.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/db/cost.migration.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not duplicate cost_per_kg field — it stays denormalized on items.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
